blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Placental Pathology?
รก (placenta) เดินทางมาพร้อมกับทารกตลอด 9 เดือน — no evaluation of a sick neonate is complete without knowing the status of its placenta (Redline 2025). 5 value-added functions: (1) identify immediately treatable processes (chorioamnionitis → EOS risk; Candida funisitis → antifungal); (2) determine underlying cause (preterm, FGR, stillbirth → pathophysiologic phenotype); (3) estimate recurrence risk (0–70% depending on lesion); (4) guide future care path (screen maternal disease, genetic testing, early delivery next pregnancy); (5) quality assurance (gold standard distinguishing true/false chorioamnionitis, abruption). เราเป็นคนสั่ง workup EOS, ดู risk sepsis, วางแผน counseling — ต้องอ่าน placenta report ได้.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
ระบุ indications สำหรับ placental submission ตาม CAP 1997 guideline (Box 25.1) — maternal, fetal/neonatal, gross abnormality
อธิบาย 4 major compartments ของ placenta: fetal circulation, maternal uterine-trophoblastic, villous trophoblast, membranes
ระบุ maternal vascular malperfusion (MVM): mechanism, histologic features, clinical associations (preeclampsia, FGR, abruption)
อธิบาย fetal vascular malperfusion (FVM): segmental vs global, predisposing factors, neonatal consequences
แยก acute chorioamnionitis (ascending) ออกจาก chronic placentitis (hematogenous): staging, organisms, histologic patterns
อธิบาย villitis of unknown etiology (VUE): mechanism, recurrence risk, clinical impact, ความแตกต่างจาก infectious placentitis
ระบุ structural/developmental lesions: placenta accreta spectrum, umbilical cord coiling anomalies, chorangioma, choriocarcinoma
เชื่อม placental lesion กับ clinical outcome: preterm labor, FGR, IUFD, HIE, cerebral palsy — lesion pattern ต่อ diagnosis
ระบุ TORCH/SARS-CoV-2/Listeria/Candida: distinctive histopathologic placental features แต่ละ organism
อธิบาย clinicopathologic correlation: โรคใดมี placental lesion pattern อะไร — reference กับ Box 25.2
1. Optimal Use of Placental Pathology Service
1.1 When to Submit the Placenta

CAP 1997 Placental Submission Guidelines (Box 25.1) — 3 Groups
Maternal indications: delivery <37 wk or >42 wk; systemic disorders (HTN, DM); peripartum fever/infection; unexplained 3rd-trimester bleeding; recurrent pregnancy complications; invasive procedures with suspected injury; thick/viscid meconium; severe oligo/polyhydramnios. Fetal/Neonatal: stillbirth/neonatal death; NICU admission; SGA (<10th) or LGA (>90th); birth depression (pH <7.0, Apgar 5-min <7, ventilation >10 min, Hct <35); neonatal seizures; suspected infection/sepsis; hydrops; multiple gestation (fused/same-sex/discordant). Gross abnormalities: structural abnormalities of disk/membranes; abnormal size/weight; umbilical cord abnormalities (long/short/hypercoiled/abnormal insertion/single artery).
Transport policy: ถ้าทารก transport มา tertiary care → bring placenta or request slides/report from referring hospital
Storage: refrigerate immediately, send WITHOUT fixative — viable ≤7 days post-delivery. If refrigeration impossible >1–2h → formalin (preserves indefinitely)
Clinical form required: pathologist needs GA, maternal history, baby weight/Apgar/malformations, specific questions — optimal interpretation depends on clinical context

Special studies rarely needed: bacterial cultures add little vs histology + neonatal blood culture; fungal stain occasionally useful; placental karyotype for confined placental mosaicism (FGR/IUFD, idiopathic)
2. Placental Structure and Pathologic Reaction Patterns
2.1 Four Major Compartments

Fetal circulation: umbilical arteries (2) → chorionic plate → stem villi → terminal villi capillaries → umbilical vein (1). Umbilical vein = sole supply of oxygenated blood; thinnest wall → most susceptible to compression

Maternal uterine-trophoblastic: maternal blood fills intervillous space via spiral arteries. Trophoblast invades and remodels spiral arteries (muscularis → fibrinoid) → ensures low-resistance perfusion. Failure = preeclampsia/FGR/abruption pathogenesis

Villous trophoblast (barrier): syncytiotrophoblast (gas exchange) + cytotrophoblast stem cells. Vasculosyncytial membranes fuse near term to facilitate exchange. Annexin-V prevents coagulation on trophoblast surface — antiphospholipid Ab displaces annexin-V
Membranes: rupture = normal delivery mechanism; premature rupture from ↑ pressure or ↓ structural integrity (infection, ischemia). Incorporate meconium/bacteria/hemosiderin from amniotic fluid

Placenta Functional Unit — Summary Diagram (Fig. 25.2 Concepts)
Fetal path: UA → chorionic plate → stem villous arteries → terminal villi capillaries → stem venules → chorionic plate veins → UV → fetus. Maternal path: decidual arteries (trophoblast-lined) → intervillous space → decidual veins. Sites of injury: (1) decidual arteriopathy → ↓ maternal perfusion; (2) massive perivillous fibrin → coats villi, ↓ gas exchange; (3) VUE → ↑ diffusion distance; (4) cord accident → global ↓ fetoplacental circulation; (5) meconium/CA → damage fetal vessels in chorionic plate; (6) segmental FVM thrombi → ↓ distal vascular bed.
3. Maternal Uterine-Trophoblastic Lesions
3.1 Maternal Vascular Malperfusion (MVM)

Mechanism: incomplete trophoblast remodeling → spiral arteries remain muscularized → susceptible to spasm, degeneration, stenosis, rupture. Chronic conditions (renal disease, HTN, collagen vascular) further compromise

Histology: accelerated villous maturation (↑ syncytial knots = syncytial nuclear aggregates); ↓ villous size for GA; intervillous fibrin; villous agglutination in 'watershed zones' between spiral arteries (see Fig. 25.3B)
Low placental weight: hallmark of chronic MVM
Villous infarction: total arterial occlusion; up to 20–25% infarction tolerated without acute fetal compromise (experimental), but remaining placenta already impaired

Delayed villous maturation: decreased vasculosyncytial membranes near term → ↓ placental reserve → susceptibility to transient hypoxic events; prototype = infant of diabetic mother; associated with IUFD (Stallmach Obstet Gynecol 2001)
3.2 Abruptio Placentae

Acute abruption: rupture of maternal arteries; most common = ischemia-reperfusion injury + secondary vascular wall dissolution (not just trauma). Strong association with vasoactive drugs: nicotine, cocaine (Acker 1983; Naeye 1977)
Preeclampsia: incompletely remodeled muscular arteries → particularly prone to rupture → abruption especially frequent
Chronic abruption: organizing marginal blood clots + chorioamniotic hemosiderin deposition + placental circumvallation; predominantly venous (not arterial) hemorrhage (Naftolin 1973)
3.3 Massive Perivillous Fibrin Deposition (Maternal Floor Infarction)
Lesion: fibrin + trophoblast-derived extracellular matrix surrounds terminal villi → ↓ intervillous circulation + gas exchange (Fig. 25.3A)
Mechanism: altered anticoagulant mechanisms in intervillous space; antiphospholipid Ab displaces annexin-V from trophoblast surface → coagulation activation
Recurrence risk: very high (up to 70%) — one of highest recurrence rates among placental lesions; prompt maternal workup (APS, thrombophilia)
4. Fetal Stromal-Vascular Lesions
4.1 Umbilical Cord Compromise

Wharton's jelly: hydrated extracellular matrix that protects umbilical vessels from compression. Compromise: ↓ hydration (MVM), excessive torsion (hypercoiling), velamentous insertion (vessels exposed to trauma)
Critical criteria for significant cord lesion: (1) local injury at proposed occlusion site + (2) distal changes reflecting compromised flow — both required
4.2 Fetal Vascular Malperfusion (FVM) — Segmental

FVM — Predisposing Factors, Histology, Consequences
Lesion: thrombotic occlusion of large chorionic plate/stem villous vessels → downstream avascular hyalinized villi (Fig. 25.3C). Predisposing factors: inflammation (VUE/chorioamnionitis); toxic damage (meconium exposure); stasis (chronic cord obstruction, fetal CHF); inherited thrombophilia (Protein C/S deficiency, factor V Leiden); APS (antiphospholipid Ab); hyperviscosity (polycythemia); maternal DM/platelet disorders. Consequences: ↓ gas exchange capacity; thromboembolic disease in fetus (stroke, renal vein thrombosis, intestinal atresia); consumptive coagulopathy (DIC/thrombocytopenia) in rare cases. Cerebral palsy (term): segmental FVM most common large-vessel lesion.

Fetal arteriolar changes: arterioles in mature intermediate villi regulate placental resistance (= parameter of Doppler umbilical artery). ↓ arterioles in autosomal trisomies; obliterated/stenosed in severe FGR

Chorangiosis: capillary bed expands by angiogenesis in response to chronic hypoxia; defined as ≥10 capillary cross-sections in significant proportion of terminal villi; associated with VUE, meconium, DM, congenital anomalies
4.3 Fetomaternal Hemorrhage
Villous capillary rupture → intervillous thrombi + fetal blood in maternal circulation; massive: hypovolemia, fetal anemia, ↑ circulating nucleated RBCs, compensatory erythropoiesis
Chronic FMH → congestive heart failure + hydrops fetalis; also: hydrops from parvovirus B19, toxoplasmosis, syphilis
Efficient re-epithelialization mechanism: villous repair restricts blood loss usually; kapillary rupture = rare acute event
5. Inflammatory Lesions — Infectious
5.1 Acute Chorioamnionitis (Ascending Infection)
Mechanism: cervicovaginal bacteria overwhelm cervical host defenses or gain access after membrane rupture → ascending infection. Most common cause of preterm labor (Hillier N Engl J Med 1988)
Response predominantly neutrophilic: membranes + chorionic plate (NOT chorionic villi). Few delivered infants are septic at birth — placenta acts as effective barrier

Chorioamnionitis Staging — Duration of Infection
Maternal response stages: Stage 1 (<6h): neutrophils enter membranous chorion + underside of chorionic plate. Stage 2 (6–24h): neutrophils infiltrate full chorionic plate + full thickness of membranes. Stage 3 (>24h): necrosis of amnion. Fetal response stages: Stage 1: transmigration in chorionic plate vessels + umbilical vein. Stage 2: umbilical arteries involved. Stage 3: neutrophils infiltrate umbilical cord stroma. Late/prolonged (days-weeks): subnecrotizing funisitis (perivascular arcs of calcific debris, glycoprotein, neovascularization) OR subacute chorioamnionitis (histiocytic component) (Ohyama Hum Pathol 2002). Subnecrotizing funisitis/subacute CA → ↑ risk chronic lung disease (BPD).
Severe fetal inflammatory response (FIR): risk factor for BPD, NEC, cranial US abnormalities, long-term neurologic impairment (Gomez Am J Obstet Gynecol 1998; Redline Arch Pathol Lab Med 1998)
5.2 Organism-Specific Patterns

rows: Neutrophilic exudates involving VILLI and intervillous space (not just membranes) — distinguishes from typical ascending infection
Microabscesses on EXTERNAL surface of umbilical cord (funisitis); also umbilical cord staining
Villous plasma cells (plasma cell villitis); intranuclear/intracytoplasmic inclusions
↑ Hofbauer cells + periarteritis + necrotizing umbilical phlebitis
Diffuse trophoblast necrosis + chronic intervillositis (Watkins Arch Pathol Lab Med 2021)
Hofbauer cell proliferation/hyperplasia + villous stromal macrophages (Rosenberg Arch Pathol Lab Med 2017)

blocks.text: 5.3 Chronic Placentitis (Hematogenous — TORCH)
Route: organisms enter placenta via maternal bloodstream. Hallmark: destructive diffuse villitis with fibrosis and calcification; may also involve chorion, decidua, intervillous space
Organisms: TORCH (Toxoplasma, rubella, CMV, HSV), Treponema pallidum, other herpesviruses (EBV, VZV), Zika, SARS-CoV-2
Transplacental infections that SPARE the placenta: HIV, HBV, parvovirus B19, enteroviruses — spread to fetus via maternofetal transfusion through interhemal barrier breaks
Intrapartum infections (birth canal — spare placenta): N. gonorrhoeae, Chlamydia, HPV, GBS, E. coli
6. Inflammatory Lesions — Graft-Versus-Host Type
6.1 Villitis of Unknown Etiology (VUE)

VUE — Mechanism, Prevalence, Impact, Recurrence
Prevalence: 5% of all term placentas. Infectious placentitis: rare (1–2/100 live births). Distinguishing features vs infectious placentitis: VUE confined to villi only (not decidua/chorion); involves fraction of villous tree; predominant cells = maternal T lymphocytes (not neutrophils); ↑ older women + multigravida; high recurrence risk. Infectious placentitis: younger women, rarely recurs (acquired immunity). Mechanism: graft-versus-host type reaction — maternal T cells respond to fetal antigens. Consequence: chronic villous stromal inflammation → ↑ diffusion distance for gas exchange (Fig. 25.3D) → FGR, stillbirth (in severe/stem vessel obliteration form). Chronic chorioamnionitis (related lesion): may contribute to idiopathic preterm labor (Kim Mod Pathol 2010).
VUE with stem vessel obliteration: large fetal vessel involvement → FGR + cerebral palsy (term); associated with Doppler abnormalities
7. Developmental and Structural Lesions
7.1 Placenta Accreta Spectrum
Mechanism: decidua regulates depth of implantation; prior surgery/uterine abnormalities → deficient decidualization → uncontrolled placental growth INTO (accreta) or THROUGH (percreta) uterine wall
Trophotropism theory: bilobation, accessory lobes, velamentous insertion, placenta previa → placenta migrates from uterine defects toward better blood supply
7.2 Umbilical Cord Coiling Anomalies

Umbilical Cord Coiling — Hypocoiling vs Hypercoiling
Normal coiling: ~1 coil/10 cm of cord. Hypocoiling (<1 coil/10 cm): associated with fetal distress, oligohydramnios, preterm delivery, IUGR, meconium-stained AF, FHR alterations, low cord pH. Hypercoiling (>3 coils/cm — 'thin cord syndrome'): constriction of umbilical vessels → perinatal asphyxia, IUFD. Noncoiled cords: risk factor for poor perinatal outcome + stillbirth (Strong Obstet Gynecol 1993). Mechanisms: early embryonic maldevelopment, dyssynchrony in vessel growth, differential blood flow, biased fetal movement (Machin Pediatr Dev Pathol 2000).
7.3 Placental Tumors
Chorangioma: most common placental tumor; benign (proliferating fetal blood vessels). Complication if large: A-V shunting → hydrops fetalis + platelet sequestration → DIC

Choriocarcinoma: malignant trophoblastic tumor; rarely arises from 2nd/3rd trimester villi; may metastasize to fetus. Maternal metastatic tumors to placenta: melanoma, adenocarcinoma. Fetal primary tumors in placenta: congenital neuroblastoma, Langerhans cell histiocytosis
7.4 Amnion Nodosum and Amniotic Bands
Amnion nodosum: deposits on amnion surface from prolonged oligohydramnios; associated with pulmonary hypoplasia (Landing Am J Obstet Gynecol 1950)
Amniotic bands: associated with fetal amputations (disruptive sequence); band formation = torn amnion + fibrous strands entrap fetal parts
7.5 Monochorionic Twin Complications
Vascular anastomoses in MC/DA twins: acute circulatory shifts → hypoxic-ischemic injury (IUFD of co-twin → acute embolic/hypotensive injury to survivor); chronic → TTTS + discordant growth
MC/MA twins: rarely TTTS but entangled umbilical cords — can cause death of one or both twins
8. Clinicopathologic Correlation (Box 25.2)

rows: Acute chorioamnionitis (infectious) — most common; abruption (acute/chronic); MVM ('accelerated maturation'); chronic chorioamnionitis (graft-vs-host)
MVM (most frequent); VUE; massive perivillous fibrin deposition (maternal floor infarction); FVM; confined placental chromosomal mosaicism
Delayed villous maturation; massive FMH; umbilical cord accident; multiple/severe combined lesions; placental exam reveals cause in 60–70% unexplained IUFD
Abruptio placentae or uterine rupture; umbilical cord accident; rupture of fetal vessels
Severe villous edema; chorionic vessel thrombi; multiple placental lesions
Segmental FVM; VUE with stem vessel obliteration; severe fetal chorioamnionitis; meconium-associated vascular necrosis; multiple placental lesions

blocks.text: 8.1 Preterm Labor

Acute chorioamnionitis = most common cause preterm labor; difficult to determine if infection precedes or follows labor onset — since CA triggers labor relatively rapidly, most cases post-prolonged PROM = secondary infection
Other preterm groups: abruption; MVM; uterine structural anomalies without placental abnormality (cervical insufficiency); chronic chorioamnionitis (idiopathic preterm labor, Kim 2010)
8.2 FGR
Constitutional FGR: small maternal size, malnutrition, anomalous fetus — no placental disease
Placental FGR — most frequent: MVM + VUE. Less frequent: FVM, massive perivillous fibrin deposition, confined placental mosaicism (Kalousek Science 1983)
8.3 IUFD

Placental exam reveals cause in 60–70% of unexplained IUFD — higher yield than autopsy (Kidron Placenta 2009). Key groups: cord accidents + delayed villous maturation (↓ placental reserve) + massive FMH
Increased circulating nucleated RBCs: marker of recurrent intermittent hypoxia (cord problems); acute FMH
8.4 CNS Injury
Preterm WMD/periventricular hemorrhagic infarction/CP: severe villous edema, chorionic vessel thrombi (chorioamnionitis-associated), multiple lesions (Redline Arch Pathol Lab Med 1998)
Term HIE: birth asphyxia → abruption, cord accident, ruptured fetal vessels; recurrent intermittent hypoxia → cord problems (↑ NRBCs); chronic uteroplacental disease → large-vessel lesions
Term CP large-vessel lesions: segmental FVM, VUE with stem vessel obliteration, severe fetal CA, meconium-associated vascular necrosis, multiple lesions (Redline Am J Obstet Gynecol 2005)
9. NICU Practical — อ่าน Placenta Report อย่าง Neonatologist
9.1 เมื่อ Report บอก 'Histologic Chorioamnionitis'
Grade severity: maternal stage 1–3 (duration <6h → >24h) + fetal stage 1–3 (umbilical vein → arteries → stroma)
Fetal inflammatory response (FIR) ≥ Stage 2: ↑↑ EOS risk, ↑ BPD, ↑ NEC, ↑ neurologic impairment → low threshold for empiric antibiotics; อย่า reassure ว่า 'ไม่มี CA' ถ้า histologic grade ยังไม่กลับมา
Subnecrotizing funisitis or subacute CA: prolonged exposure (days-weeks) → ↑ CLD/BPD risk significantly — counseling family ล่วงหน้า
Specific patterns: villous involvement (Listeria) → septic workup + ampicillin urgently; umbilical cord microabscesses (Candida) → fluconazole/antifungal
9.2 เมื่อ Report บอก 'Maternal Vascular Malperfusion'
แสดงว่า: uteroplacental insufficiency ชัดเจน → FGR expected; เสี่ยง NEC (intestinal hypoperfusion), polycythemia, hypoglycemia, hypothermia
Next pregnancy recurrence risk: ต้องแจ้ง OB → screen HTN/renal disease/APS; enhanced antenatal surveillance next pregnancy
9.3 เมื่อ Report บอก 'Fetal Vascular Malperfusion (FVM)'
Evaluate for thrombophilia workup (protein C/S, factor V Leiden, APS), DM
Watch for: stroke (cranial US/MRI), NEC, renal vein thrombosis, thrombocytopenia → coagulation studies. Neonatal coagulopathy if large placental FVM
FVM บน placenta report ไม่ได้หมายความว่ามีแน่นอน — ต้อง clinical correlation ร่วมกับ imaging
9.4 เมื่อ Report บอก 'Villitis of Unknown Etiology (VUE)'
High recurrence (graft-vs-host) → แจ้ง OB: เพิ่ม antenatal surveillance next pregnancy
Evaluate FGR severity; ถ้า stem vessel obliteration → ↑↑ risk CP/neurologic impairment (MRI brain พิจารณา)
TORCH workup in neonate ไม่จำเป็น ถ้า pathology ชัดว่า VUE (maternal T-cells, no inclusions, no organisms) — infectious placentitis ต่างกัน
10. Differential Diagnosis — Placental Lesion Patterns
11. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — ส่ง Placenta ทุก NICU Admission — ไม่มีข้อยกเว้น
CAP guideline 1997: NICU admission = indication สำหรับ placental submission. Placenta เดินทางมากับทารกและรู้เรื่องราวทั้งหมดของ 9 เดือน — placental exam reveals cause of IUFD in 60–70% of cases, more than autopsy (Kidron Placenta 2009). ถ้าทารก transport มา ไม่มี placenta → ขอ slides + report จาก referring hospital. Refrigerate; send without fixative; valid ≤7 days. Clinical form ให้ครบ — pathologist ต้องรู้ GA, Apgar, specific question.

PEARL 2 — Fetal Stage 3 Chorioamnionitis = Funisitis = ↑↑ Morbidity
Fetal inflammatory response stage 3 (neutrophils infiltrate umbilical cord stroma) หรือ subnecrotizing funisitis (days-weeks exposure) → ↑ BPD, NEC, cranial US abnormalities, long-term neurologic impairment (Redline 1998; Gomez 1998). ไม่ใช่แค่ 'sepsis risk' — เป็น systemic fetal inflammatory response syndrome (FIRS). FIRS = ↑ fetal IL-6 >11 pg/mL. ใน NICU: low threshold empiric antibiotics; close monitor respiratory, gut, neuro; early BPD management. อย่าดู maternal stage เดียว — fetal stage สำคัญกว่า.

PEARL 3 — Listeria จะเห็นใน VILLI, Candida จะเห็นที่ CORD SURFACE
Pattern recognition ช่วย diagnosis: Listeria monocytogenes → neutrophilic exudates involving villi + intervillous space (ต่างจาก typical ascending CA ที่อยู่แค่ membranes + chorionic plate); Candida albicans → microabscesses on EXTERNAL surface of umbilical cord. ทั้งสองตัวนี้ rare แต่ lethal ถ้าไม่ treat: Listeria → ampicillin IV urgently (gentamicin synergy); Candida → fluconazole IV (เชื้อ fungi resistant to cephalosporins). ถ้า placenta report ระบุ pattern เหล่านี้ → เปลี่ยน antibiotic ทันที.

PEARL 4 — Maternal Floor Infarction = Recurrence 70% — Maternal Workup เร่งด่วน
Massive perivillous fibrin deposition ('maternal floor infarction') = หนึ่งใน placental lesion ที่มี recurrence risk สูงสุด (ถึง 70%). Mechanism: antiphospholipid Ab displaces annexin-V → coagulation activation ใน intervillous space. ดังนั้น: เมื่อ report บอก lesion นี้ → maternal APS workup (anticardiolipin, anti-β2GPI, lupus anticoagulant); thrombophilia screen. ถ้า positive → low-dose aspirin + LMWH next pregnancy; high-risk OB referral. Neonate: FGR + possible IUGR complications (NEC, hypoglycemia, polycythemia).

PEARL 5 — 'Delayed Villous Maturation' ใน Infant of Diabetic Mother = ↑ IUFD Risk
Placentas of IDM (and post-term) show delayed villous maturation: decreased vasculosyncytial membranes near term → ↓ placental reserve → susceptibility to transient hypoxic events → IUFD (Stallmach Obstet Gynecol 2001). นี่คือเหตุผลที่ IDM ต้อง deliver ก่อน EDD และ NST สม่ำเสมอ. ใน NICU: IDM ที่คลอดด้วย unexplained late decelerations/stillbirth → placental report ควร document delayed villous maturation. มีผลต่อ counseling next pregnancy (tight glycemic control + earlier delivery).
12. Evidence Update — Key Studies
Langston C et al Arch Pathol Lab Med 1997;121:449: CAP practice guideline for placental examination — indications (Box 25.1); consensus task force of pathologists, obstetricians, neonatologists
Redline RW et al Pediatr Dev Pathol 2003;6:435: amniotic fluid infection: nosology and reproducibility of placental reaction patterns (chorioamnionitis staging)
Gomez R et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 1998;179:194: fetal inflammatory response syndrome (FIRS): fetal IL-6 >11 pg/mL; severe neonatal morbidities (BPD, NEC, neurologic) — defining study for FIRS concept
Hillier SL et al N Engl J Med 1988;319:972: case-control study; chorioamniotic infection and histologic chorioamnionitis in prematurity — acute CA = most common cause preterm labor
Ohyama M et al Hum Pathol 2002;33:183: subnecrotizing funisitis and subacute chorioamnionitis: duration markers; correlation with chronic lung disease

Redline RW et al Arch Pathol Lab Med 1998;122:1091: placental lesions associated with neurologic impairment and cerebral palsy in VLBW infants: villous edema, chorionic vessel thrombi, multiple lesions

Redline RW & O'Riordan MA Arch Pathol Lab Med 2000;124:1785: placental lesions associated with CP and neurologic impairment after term birth: large-vessel FVM, VUE, meconium-associated vascular necrosis
Redline RW Am J Obstet Gynecol 2005;192:452: severe fetal placental vascular lesions in term infants with neurologic impairment; FVM most significant
Redline RW Hum Pathol 2008;39:1378: elevated circulating fetal NRBCs and placental pathology in term infants developing CP — recurrent intermittent hypoxia marker
Kidron D et al Placenta 2009;30:700: placental findings in fetal death 23–40 wk (n=120): cause identified in 60–70% by placental exam — higher than autopsy
Kim CJ et al Mod Pathol 2010;23:1000: chronic chorioamnionitis (graft-vs-host type): frequency, clinical significance, features — associated with idiopathic preterm labor
Stallmach T et al Obstet Gynecol 2001;97:505: delayed villous maturation and late fetal mortality: decreased vasculosyncytial membranes → ↓ placental reserve; rescue by birth concept
Kalousek DK Science 1983;221:665: chromosomal mosaicism confined to placenta (CPM) in human conceptions: FGR + IUFD etiology
Rand JH & Wu X-X Thromb Haemost 1999;82:649: antiphospholipid-mediated disruption of annexin-V antithrombotic shield: mechanism for thrombosis in APS; explains maternal floor infarction
Watkins JC et al Arch Pathol Lab Med 2021;145:1341: defining severe SARS-CoV-2 placentitis: diffuse trophoblast necrosis + chronic intervillositis pattern
Rosenberg AZ et al Arch Pathol Lab Med 2017;141:43: Zika virus placental pathology: Hofbauer cell proliferation + hyperplasia; villous stromal macrophage involvement
Machin GA et al Pediatr Dev Pathol 2000;3:462: abnormal umbilical cord coiling associated with adverse perinatal outcomes: hypo- and hypercoiling patterns
Redline RW et al Mod Pathol 2021;34:1074: four major patterns of placental injury: stepwise guide for understanding and implementing 2016 Amsterdam consensus on placental pathology nomenclature
13. Self-Check Q&A
Q: Placental examination ให้ 'value added' อะไร 5 อย่าง ในการดูแล neonate?

ตอบ: (1) Identify immediately treatable processes: CA + FIR → EOS risk; Candida funisitis → antifungal. (2) Determine underlying cause: pathophysiologic phenotype ของ preterm/FGR/IUFD. (3) Estimate recurrence risk: 0–70% depending on lesion. (4) Guide future care path: maternal workup, genetic testing, high-risk referral. (5) Quality assurance: gold standard distinguish true/false chorioamnionitis, abruption; identify subacute/chronic disorders predisposing to HIE/CP.
Q: Chorioamnionitis fetal stage 1, 2, 3 หมายถึงอะไร และทำไมสำคัญกว่า maternal stage?

ตอบ: Stage 1: neutrophil transmigration in chorionic plate vessels + umbilical vein. Stage 2: umbilical arteries. Stage 3: neutrophil infiltration of umbilical cord stroma. สำคัญกว่า maternal stage เพราะ fetal inflammatory response (FIRS) เชื่อมกับ BPD, NEC, cranial US abnormalities, neurologic impairment โดยตรง (Gomez 1998; Redline 1998). Maternal stage บอก duration ของ infection; fetal stage บอก fetal systemic response.
Q: VUE ต่างจาก infectious placentitis อย่างไร และควร workup TORCH ใน neonate เมื่อไหร่?

ตอบ: VUE: villi only; maternal T lymphocytes; high recurrence; older/multigravida; no organism. Infectious placentitis: diffuse; villi + chorion + decidua; specific histopathologic patterns (plasma cells = CMV; cord pseudocysts = toxoplasma; etc.); younger; rarely recurs. TORCH workup indicated: only for infectious placentitis pattern — ไม่ใช่ VUE. VUE = maternal T-cells, no inclusions, no organisms → TORCH ไม่จำเป็น.
Q: Placental pattern ใดที่เชื่อมกับ cerebral palsy ใน term infant? และต่างจาก preterm อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Term CP: segmental FVM, VUE with stem vessel obliteration, severe fetal chorioamnionitis, meconium-associated vascular necrosis, multiple lesions. Preterm CP: severe villous edema, chorionic vessel thrombi (chorioamnionitis-associated), multiple lesions (Redline 2000, 2005). Key difference: term CP ↑ large-vessel lesions (FVM/VUE stem); preterm CP ↑ small-vessel/inflammatory lesions + edema.
Q: Placenta report ระบุ 'massive perivillous fibrin deposition' — ควร action อะไร?

ตอบ: Maternal workup เร่งด่วน: APS (anticardiolipin, anti-β2GPI, lupus anticoagulant); thrombophilia screen. Recurrence risk up to 70% — ต้องแจ้ง OB + family. ถ้า APS positive → next pregnancy: low-dose aspirin + LMWH + high-risk OB. Neonate: FGR complications (NEC, hypoglycemia, polycythemia). Mechanism: annexin-V displacement by APA → coagulation activation in intervillous space (Rand 1999).
Q: Placenta biopsy พบ Listeria vs Candida: histologic pattern และ antibiotic ที่ต้องเปลี่ยน?

ตอบ: Listeria: neutrophilic exudates involving villi + intervillous space (ต่างจาก typical ascending CA). Action: ampicillin + gentamicin (synergistic); ampicillin ครอบคลุม Listeria ที่ cephalosporin ไม่ครอบ. Candida: microabscesses on EXTERNAL surface of umbilical cord. Action: fluconazole IV หรือ micafungin ถ้า resistant; bacteria เปลี่ยน coverage ให้ครอบ fungi. ทั้งสองตัวนี้ rare แต่ lethal — อย่ารอ culture กลับมาก่อน.
Q: Umbilical cord hypercoiling กับ hypocoiling: define, risk, คลินิก?

ตอบ: Hypocoiling (<1 coil/10 cm): fetal distress, oligohydramnios, preterm, IUGR, meconium-stained AF, FHR alterations, ↓ cord pH. Hypercoiling (>3 coils/cm): thin cord syndrome → constriction umbilical vessels → perinatal asphyxia, IUFD. Noncoiled: stillbirth risk (Strong 1993). Normal: ~1 coil/10 cm. Cord coiling anomaly บน placenta report → correlate กับ FHR tracing + cord pH + neonatal condition.
Q: Delayed villous maturation คืออะไร และ infant กลุ่มไหนเสี่ยงที่สุด?

ตอบ: Delayed villous maturation: decreased vasculosyncytial membranes near term → ↓ gas exchange efficiency → ↓ placental reserve → susceptibility to transient hypoxic events → IUFD. Highest risk: infant of diabetic mother (IDM) + post-term. Mechanism: DM → impaired placental maturation. Consequence: near-term/post-term IUFD unexplained; neonatal asphyxia without antecedent FHR problem. Neonatology implication: IDM → deliver ≤39–40 wk; strict NST monitoring; do NOT dismiss 'unexplained late decelerations' in IDM (Stallmach 2001).
Q: FVM บน placenta report: ต้อง workup อะไรใน neonate?

ตอบ: FVM (fetal vascular malperfusion) = thrombotic occlusion ของ fetal vessels → ↓ vascular bed + thromboembolic disease ใน fetus. Workup: (1) Cranial US/MRI: ischemic stroke, infarct; (2) Coagulation studies: PT, APTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer (DIC/consumptive coagulopathy); (3) Thrombophilia: Protein C/S activity, factor V Leiden, APS (if maternal indication); (4) Renal US (renal vein thrombosis); (5) Abdominal US (hepatic thrombosis, NEC). Platelet count daily ถ้า FVM extensive.
Q: ทำไม placenta exam reveal cause of IUFD ได้มากกว่า autopsy?

ตอบ: Placenta exam identifies cause in 60–70% of unexplained IUFD (Kidron Placenta 2009) — higher than autopsy alone. เหตุผล: placenta แสดง subacute + chronic processes ที่ autopsy มักพลาด (cord accidents ที่ pathologic criteria specific; delayed villous maturation; chronic MVM; VUE). Autopsy เก่งเรื่อง acute lethal anomalies แต่ chronic placental disease ต้องดูจาก placenta. ดังนั้น: IUFD ทุกราย → both placental exam + autopsy if possible, ไม่ใช่ autopsy เดียว.

Day 123 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.26
บทต่อไป: Chapter 26 — Anesthesia for Labor and Delivery. เตรียมตัว: neuraxial anesthesia (epidural, spinal, CSE) — mechanism, maternal effects, fetal/neonatal effects; systemic opioids (fentanyl, remifentanil) vs neuraxial: ผลต่อ neonatal respiratory depression; general anesthesia ใน emergency CS: RSI protocol, aspiration risk, intubation failure; hypotension จาก spinal → uteroplacental insufficiency → fetal acidosis; neonatal resuscitation after maternal anesthesia; epidural fever → false-positive CA workup in neonate — เรื่องที่ neonatologist ต้องรู้เพื่อ interpret neonatal status post-delivery.
